run_focus: Day-to-day lab workflows with heavy screenshot scrutiny. Emphasize inventory placement (bottles on lab benches, aliquot tubes in freezer boxes with grid positions), creating things, renaming, moving between locations, linking objects across pages, reading/printing protocols, lab notebook entries with photos, and marking items as needing reorder. Every action must be screenshotted before AND after, and every screenshot must be Read and visually evaluated for correctness, clarity, and consistency.

personas.name: Vianney Ahn
personas.focus: DNA extraction, sample prep, pistachio pangenome
personas.workflow: notebook-heavy, daily entries, gel images, sample tracking, aliquoting into freezer boxes
tasks: Create daily notebook entry with today's extraction results (3+ samples, NanoDrop values)
Upload gel image from today's run, annotate lanes with sample names
Add 3 new sample tubes and place each in a specific freezer box grid cell
Check inventory for DNeasy kits, mark as need-more if low
Search protocols for 'PCR' and follow the genotyping protocol end-to-end
Store extracted DNA aliquots in -80 freezer box, record grid positions (A1, A2, B1)
Edit yesterday's notebook entry to add forgotten results
Cross-reference a protocol from within a notebook entry using [[wikilinks]]
Search for a reagent she knows exists but can't remember the exact name
Reproduce a batch of 10 aliquot tubes from a parent sample, place them in a new freezer box

Duplicate an existing protocol and modify buffer volumes

Create a new project page for a grant and fill in all sections with wikilinks
Add 5 inventory items for a new kit that just arrived, place each on the right shelf
Make a new bottle of ethanol (concept -> bottle instance) and place on a lab bench
Create a new freezer box, place it on a specific shelf, add 5 tubes into it
Edit an existing wiki page to add safety information
Create a wiki page for a new piece of equipment with exact location details
Use the locations picker to move 3 items from one shelf to another

personas.name: Dr. Monroe
personas.focus: oversight, bulletin board, dashboard review, project status
personas.workflow: dashboard -> check updates -> review notebooks -> check inventory needs -> bulletin
tasks: Review dashboard: are stats correct? Recent updates make sense? Badges showing?
Edit the bulletin board with new lab meeting time and attachments
Check all project pages -- do they have content? Are wikilinks resolving?
Review a student's notebook entry end-to-end -- is it complete? Images rendering?
Check the knowledge graph -- are connections visible? Can you filter by type?
Look at inventory needs-ordering list -- is it accurate? Toggle the flag on an item
Navigate to sample tracker, filter by status, check counts match frontmatter

behavior_modifiers.name: rushed
behavior_modifiers.description: Types fast, doesn't wait for saves, clicks away quickly. Tests race conditions and unsaved state.

behavior_modifiers.name: careful
behavior_modifiers.description: Reads every page, verifies rendered output after every save, checks content via GitHub API. Catches stale render bugs.

behavior_modifiers.name: confused
behavior_modifiers.description: Clicks wrong buttons, uses search with typos, tries to save without required fields. Tests error handling.

behavior_modifiers.name: thorough
behavior_modifiers.description: Fills every optional field, uploads multiple images, creates deeply nested content with many wikilinks. Tests limits.

behavior_modifiers.name: mobile
behavior_modifiers.description: Uses 375x812 viewport throughout. Tests responsive layout, touch targets, bottom nav, image upload on mobile.

behavior_modifiers.name: adversarial
behavior_modifiers.description: HTML/script injection, 500+ char strings, emoji-heavy names, Unicode edge cases. Tests input sanitization.

behavior_modifiers.name: impatient
behavior_modifiers.description: Double-clicks save, refreshes mid-save, back-button after submit. Tests interruption handling.

behavior_modifiers.name: explorer
behavior_modifiers.description: URL manipulation (?doc=nonexistent), opens pages in unexpected order, deep-links to deleted items. Tests error states.

behavior_modifiers.name: ui_critic
behavior_modifiers.description: Focuses on visual coherence -- does the pill color match the type? Is the breadcrumb correct? Is the grid cell rendering the right child? Is spacing consistent? Screenshot-heavy.

behavior_modifiers.name: cross_linker
behavior_modifiers.description: Obsessed with links -- follows every wikilink, verifies bidirectional backlinks, checks that object pills render with correct icon+title, tests [[ autocomplete everywhere.

scenario_cards: Ethanol bottle day: Inventory -> Add new bottle of Ethanol Absolute (from ethanol-absolute concept). Give it a lot number, expiration, assign location to lab-bench. Save. Verify it appears in the concept's 'physical bottles' list. Open the lab bench location -- verify the new bottle is a child. Screenshot every step.

Aliquot day: Take an existing sample (e.g. sample-pistachio-4). Create 6 new tube instances named tube-pistachio-4-aliquot-1 through -6, each pointing to the sample via of:. Pick an existing box OR create a new box box-pistachio-aliquots (grid 9x9) on shelf-minus80-a-1. Assign each tube to grid positions A1 through A6. Save. Open box popup, confirm all 6 aliquots render in the grid.

New freezer box day: Create new box 'Library Prep Kit Storage' (grid 10x10) on an existing shelf. Add 3 tubes into it at specific positions. Verify the box appears in the shelf children list, the tubes appear in the box grid, and each tube shows the right breadcrumb.
Reorder day: Scan inventory, pick 3 items that are actually low, toggle need-more on each. Verify dashboard needs-ordering count updates. Toggle one back off and verify it decrements.

Lab notebook full day: Create a new notebook entry under your own folder. Add a title, body with 3 section headers, a table of 4 columns x 6 rows, a callout, a wikilink to an existing protocol, upload 2 photos (plant growth, gel), annotate one photo with a label. Save. Reload. Verify all content renders correctly.

Print protocol day: Open a protocol with images. Click print. Verify the print preview opens, images load fully (not blank), pagination doesn't clip figures, headings stay with their sections. Screenshot the print preview.
Rename + relink day: Rename an existing protocol. Verify any wikilinks pointing to the old slug still work (redirects or auto-updated). Check backlinks in the connections panel.

Equipment move day: Pick 3 inventory items currently on shelf A. Use the locations picker to move all 3 to shelf B. Verify the tree view updates on lab-map page. Verify the old shelf no longer lists them as children.

New user onboarding: Create a new person card (frontmatter type=person, name, role, focus). Create a notebook folder for them. Create their first entry. Verify the person appears on the People page and their notebook sorts correctly.

Concept+instance linking: Pick a reagent concept with zero bottles. Create 2 physical bottle instances. Verify the concept's popup column 3 now lists both bottles. Verify inventory table hides the individual bottles under the concept row.

Cross-page wikilink crawl: From the dashboard, follow a link to a protocol. From the protocol, follow a wikilink to a reagent. From the reagent to a bottle. From the bottle to a location. Each step: screenshot + evaluate breadcrumb + verify page loads correctly.
Photo-heavy notebook: Upload 5 images to one notebook entry. Annotate 3 (boxes + labels). Resize 2 smaller. Save. Reload. Verify all 5 render at the right sizes, annotations visible, no broken paths.
Mobile lab notebook: On mobile viewport, create a notebook entry with a photo + text. Verify the Aa mobile toolbar works, the fab button layout, the image upload from mobile camera input.
Bulk sample tracker: Add 10 samples in rapid succession with varying status. Filter by status. Verify counts. Check a random sample's full detail page.
Search every surface: Global search for an obscure term (e.g. 'DNeasy' or a rare reagent name). Verify it returns hits across inventory, protocols, notebooks, wiki with correct context snippets.
Backlinks sanity: Pick any object with known inbound references (e.g. a sample referenced by tubes). Open its popup. Verify all backlinks listed, each one clickable, each destination loads.
Calendar add/delete: Add a new lab block at a specific hour. Verify it appears in the week grid. Delete it. Verify it disappears.
Waste container: Create a new waste container for ethanol-contaminated gloves. Verify days-held starts at 0 and updates on reload.

screenshot_protocol.rule_1: Every click, save, navigation, or form submit MUST have a before AND after screenshot.
screenshot_protocol.rule_2: Save all cycle screenshots under /tmp/qa-screenshots/cycleN/ (create the dir).
screenshot_protocol.rule_3: Name screenshots descriptively: cycleN-stepM-<persona>-<action>.png e.g. cycle9-03-vianney-aliquot-box-popup.png
screenshot_protocol.rule_4: After taking each screenshot, READ it with the Read tool. Do NOT skip. Evaluate: is the UI rendering correctly? Is the right object loaded? Are pills colored right? Are icons right? Is the breadcrumb correct? Is there overflow, clipping, or missing text?
screenshot_protocol.rule_5: Write a visual evaluation string per screenshot into the cycle's workflows_tested entry under screenshots: [{path, what_it_shows, evaluation, issues_found}].
screenshot_protocol.rule_6: If a screenshot reveals a visual issue (even a cosmetic one), log it as a bug with severity: visual. Attempt a fix if in scope (app/ or docs/ only).

workflows_tested.task: Created new 9x9 box-pistachio-aliquots on shelf-minus80-a-1, then 6 aliquot tubes (A1-A6) referencing sample-pistachio-4 via wikilinks. Verified breadcrumbs, grid placement, hierarchy tree, and backlinks from sample popup.

screenshots.shows: Lab Map page with Locations tree expanded to room/freezer/shelf level
screenshots.evaluation: Tree renders cleanly. Robbins Hall 0170 expanded; -80C Freezer A shows 3 items; Shelf 1 (2 items), Shelf 2 (2 items), Shelf 3, 4C Fridge (14 items).
issues_found: Top nav 'Lab Map' label is truncated to 'Lab M' under the user menu — minor visual issue but consistent across all screenshots
The 'Map design in progress' SVG placeholder has overlapping text where 'Map design' overlays 'Bench 1' and 'in progress' overlays 'Bench 2' — looks awkward

screenshots.shows: Pistachio Tree #4 sample popup with fields panel, body, and 3 references in col 3
screenshots.evaluation: Three-column layout renders correctly. Fields show Name/Sample ID/Species/Project/Lead/Sequencing Type/Status. Body has correctly-colored wikilink pills (Pistachio Leaf 1/2 in teal=Tube, Pistachio Tree #4 in purple=Sample). Right column lists 3 REFERENCES (DNA Extract 1, Leaf 1, Leaf 2).

screenshots.shows: Shelf 1 popup with breadcrumb and contents (Box A, Pistachio DNA Box)
screenshots.evaluation: Breadcrumb chain Robbins Hall 0170 / -80C Freezer A / Shelf 1 renders correctly with type-colored pills. Contents pane lists 2 child boxes plus Add item button. Fields panel shows Type=Shelf, Parent=-80C Freezer A.

screenshots.shows: Lab map after creating new box; Shelf 1 child count incremented from 2 to 3 items
screenshots.evaluation: patchObjectIndex correctly updates the tree counter without a deploy. Tree still has the same items expanded.

screenshots.shows: New empty 9x9 box popup with 81 dashed empty cells labeled A1-I9
screenshots.evaluation: Grid renders correctly with column headers 1-9 and row headers A-I. All 81 cells dashed (empty state). Header reads '9 × 9 · 0 items'. Body wikilinks render correctly: Pistachio Tree #4 (purple Sample pill), Shelf 1 (grey-blue Shelf pill).
issues_found: Breadcrumb wraps to two lines and ends with a dangling trailing slash ('Pistachio Aliquots Box /' with nothing after). Cosmetic but odd.

screenshots.shows: Same box popup after creating 6 tubes; row A cells 1-6 occupied (teal), 7-9 empty
screenshots.evaluation: Header now reads '9 × 9 · 6 items'. Row A shows 6 teal-filled cells with 'P4-' label fragments visible. Cells A7-A9 stay empty. Other rows untouched. Grid correctly reflects new children via patched object index.
issues_found: Cell labels are truncated to 'P4-' — the second line of label_2 ('4/21') is hidden. Acceptable given the small cell size, but worth noting for label-design choices.

screenshots.shows: Tube popup with full 5-level breadcrumb chain
screenshots.evaluation: Breadcrumb chain wraps cleanly: Robbins Hall 0170 / -80C Freezer A / Shelf 1 / Pistachio Aliquots Box / Pistachio 4 Aliquot 3. All pill colors match type system. Position field shows A3, label_2 shows 'P4-A3 / 4/21'. Body wikilinks render correctly.

screenshots.shows: Sample popup AFTER fix: now shows 10 REFERENCES instead of 3 — all 6 new aliquots + the new box + the 3 original tubes
screenshots.evaluation: Fix verified. patchLinkIndex correctly mirrors the body's outbound wikilinks into a localStorage overlay so backlinks appear immediately without waiting for a redeploy. References now: Aliquot 1-6 (Tube), Pistachio Aliquots Box (Box), DNA Extract 1, Leaf 1, Leaf 2.

screenshots.shows: Shelf 1 popup now lists 3 children including the new Pistachio Aliquots Box
screenshots.evaluation: Parent->children relationship via the patched object index works correctly. Box A, Pistachio Aliquots Box, Pistachio DNA Box all listed.

screenshots.shows: Filtered lab-map tree showing the full hierarchy from Robbins Hall down to all 6 aliquot tubes with grid positions
screenshots.evaluation: Showcase view: tree filter 'aliquot' walks down Room -> Freezer -> Shelf -> Box -> 6 Tubes, each tube row shows its grid position (A1-A6) on the right. Box shows '9x9' grid label and '6 items' count.

bugs.summary: Backlinks panel did not include newly-created files until the next site deploy
bugs.evidence: step10 screenshot from initial run showed only 3 REFERENCES on sample-pistachio-4 popup despite 6 new tubes wikilinking to it
bugs.root_cause: fetchLinkIndex() loaded the static link-index.json built by scripts/build-object-index.py and had no localStorage patch layer like fetchObjectIndex did

fixes: Added patchLinkIndex / removeFromLinkIndex / extractWikilinkTargets in app/js/github-api.js with a localStorage overlay applied in fetchLinkIndex; wired patchLinkIndex into the editor save flow in app/js/editor-modal.js
observations: 5-level breadcrumb (Room/Freezer/Shelf/Box/Tube) renders cleanly with type-colored pills
Object-index patches cascade correctly to tree counters and Contents lists without redeploy
Type system colors are consistent across pills, popup headers, and grid cells (Box brown, Tube teal, Shelf grey-blue, Freezer light-blue, Sample purple, Room dark)
Cosmetic issue: breadcrumb wraps to two lines for deep chains and leaves a trailing slash with no following pill
Cosmetic issue: top nav 'Lab Map' is truncated to 'Lab M' (likely a label-width issue with the user menu)
Cosmetic issue: Map design placeholder has overlapping text labels

bugs_found: BACKLINKS-LAG (functional, fixed in cycle 1): newly-created or freshly-edited files were missing from the backlinks panel of every wikilink target until the next deploy
NAV-TRUNCATION (cosmetic, open): top nav 'Lab Map' tab label appears truncated to 'Lab M' on 1440-wide viewport
MAP-PLACEHOLDER-OVERLAP (cosmetic, open): SVG placeholder for the floor-plan has overlapping text labels
BREADCRUMB-DANGLING-SLASH (cosmetic, open): popup breadcrumb wraps to two lines and leaves a trailing '/' with nothing after on deep chains
fixes_applied: Live link-index patching for instant backlinks (app/js/github-api.js + app/js/editor-modal.js, commit 642579c)
improvements_proposed: Truncate label_2 only to its first line for grid-cell display so 'P4-A1' shows without losing the date line, OR shrink the date to a corner badge
Either omit the trailing breadcrumb slash on wrapped lines or include the current item as a final pill on the next line
notes: Fresh run started 2026-04-21. Prior 8-cycle run archived to tests/qa-state.archive-2026-04-12.json.
Screenshot discipline is the PRIMARY success criterion this run -- take many, READ each one, evaluate visually, log visual issues.

Cycle 1: Vianney + ui_critic + Aliquot day. Created box-pistachio-aliquots-* (9x9) on shelf-minus80-a-1, then 6 tubes at A1-A6. All artifacts cleaned up at end of cycle. Backlinks bug found and fixed; deployed; re-verified.
Phase advanced to 'functional' from 'exploration' because cycle 1 was a full day-to-day CRUD scenario.
